programme.title: Bonacini's Italy
programme.desc: Famed Chef Michael Bonacini celebrates the food of the Italian Riviera as he shows viewers delicious dishes from Liguria.

programme.title: Bonacini's Italy
programme.desc: Emilia Romagna is the culinary heart of Italy and Chef Michael Bonacini shows viewers how to make some of the region's most delicious dishes.

programme.title: Bonacini's Italy
programme.desc: Renowned Chef Michael Bonacini takes viewers on a culinary tour of the Italian region of Trentino-Alto Adige without leaving his own kitchen.

programme.title: Bonacini's Italy
programme.desc: Charming host Chef Michael Bonacini explores the mountainous region of Basilicata with a feast fit for any celebration. Beginning with an antipasto that caters to the region's southern heat, Michael stuffs crunchy sweet peppers with ripe tomatoes, savoury anchovies, and fresh oregano. Moving on to the primo, Michael cooks up a creamy Vermicelli Frittata. Using leftover pasta, he combines eggs with cheese, anchovies, and chili flakes for extra flavour to make a classic Basilicata dish. Michael cooks up Pollo Alla Potentina, a traditional meal from the Feast of Saint Gerard. This hearty chicken stew is sure to heat up your core, using hot peppers as the base of the meal. For his final dish, Michael combines the rich tastes of pomegranate, walnuts, and dark chocolate to create his unique Sweet Wheat Bowl.

programme.title: Up the Dish
programme.desc: Up the Dish host Micah Trainor tries her hand at an Italian original - Veal Piccata! Lemony, buttery, capERS-y veal melts in the mouth after being pounded supERS-thin and seared to perfection. Poppy Sandler's sideways switch-up is a Caper Almond Crusted Halibut, sizzled and baked until gorgeously golden-brown and flaky, then garnished with chives and pea shoots. Next, Missy Hui goes Greek with her vegetarian Avgolemono, a silky-smooth citrusy soup starring lemon and egg, garnished with orzo and shimeji mushrooms. Tiera Singh ties it all together with her Sumac Veal Sweetbreads with a rough chopped caperberry-olive tapenade - browned veal sweetbreads are sprinkled with tart sumac and laid on a bed of tapenade, then drizzled with a sweet 'n' sour pomegranate molasses vinaigrette.

programme.title: Up the Dish
programme.desc: First up on this episode of Up the Dish is Poppy Sandler's Borscht straight from the Eastern Bloc! A mix of roasted and simmered beets adds complexity to the creamy soup - and don't forget that vivid burgundy hue. Tiera Singh swaps soup for a sandwich with her Beet-Stained Trout Gravlax, pairing the eye-catching seafood with zesty lemon-dill cream cheese and rye pumpernickel bread. Next, Micah Trainor mixes up a vegetarian Roasted Beet Salad paired with crackly Seed Brittle and a simple sweet dressing; it's a crunchy, earthy delight! Missy Hui's three-layer Red Beet Velvet Cake is a true triple threat. Red beet puree gives this cake its vermillion colour - and pairing it with tangy goat cheese icing... well, it just makes sense!

programme.title: Up the Dish
programme.desc: In this episode of Up the Dish, host Micah Trainor takes a stab at Cassoulet with buttery white beans, duck leg confit, and flavourful French sausage - and the result is mouth-wateringly good! The pressure's on, but Tiera Singh doesn't let up with her Chinese Long Bean Stir Fry. Succulent sausage, delicious duck breast, and flavorful vegetables come together for a truly sumptuous dish! For her vegetarian variant, Missy Hui must move things up a notch, but how? Agedashi Tofu! Breaded crispy semi-firm tofu is doused in a dashi, gochujang, and mirin mixture for the ultimate umami mouthful. Poppy Sandler goes above and beyond with her Chocolate-crusted Elk Loin, seared and drizzled with a decadent Coffee- Blueberry Demi-Glace - sound strange? Tastes terrific!

programme.title: Up the Dish
programme.desc: How do you make any lasagna recipe better? Ask Tiera Singh to take it up, of course! Her 'Nonna-style' bechamel and Bolognese-based Lasagna is topped with melty mozzarella for a luscious and luxuriant dish. Tiera tosses it over to Poppy Sandler, whose sideways Seafood Lasagna mixes up mussels, shrimp, and a briny bechamel sauce - yum! Next, Micah Trainor makes a dressed-up vegetarian version of the classic - her Zucchini-Mushroom Lasagna Rolls with tangy sundried tomatoes, savoury ricotta cheese, and earthy mushroom duxelles is simply superlative. Missy Hui hits the sweet spot with her Chocolate Raspberry Dessert Lasagna; layers of rich white chocolate, thick raspberry coulis, and velvety mascarpone cheese come together for a dessert that's in the stratosphere!

programme.title: Up the Dish
programme.desc: Host Poppy Sandler kicks off Up the Dish with her version of mom's spaghetti - a scrumptious Spaghetti Pomodoro that takes it to the top! Next up to the plate is Missy Hui, who hits it out of the park with her heavenly Bun Viet, a sweet and sour vermicelli noodle bowl and citrusy lemongrass grilled pork combo. Then, Tiera Singh takes up the challenge with her vegetarian Miso Ramen - an umami flavour bomb of miso, mirin, and even more noodle goodness. But there's nowhere to go but up with Micah's Lobster Cantonese Chow Mein, an elegantly upscaled rendition of Chinese noodles with bright ginger, tender gai lan, and crisp chow mein, all coated in an exquisite black bean sauce.

programme.title: Up the Dish
programme.desc: Get ready for a not-so-raw deal in this episode of Up the Dish! Host Poppy Sandler sets things in motion and slices up a simple, elegant Sashimi with tangy Ponzu and tart Wasabi Mayo on the side. Missy Hui gets carnivorous with Lamb Carpaccio and Dukkah - delicate raw lamb and a Middle Eastern-style dip served with some delicious pita! But wait, what's a vegetarian to do with all this meat? Tiera Singh's got the answer - island-style Poke Bowls, with fruit instead of fish. Refreshing watermelon is marinated in a savoury, soy-based sauce and served with rice. For the finale, Micah Trainor Up('s) the Dish with her Scallop Tartare and Beet Chips, combining raw scallops with ultra-decadent truffle oil and sour cream.

programme.title: Cook the Books
programme.desc: Chef-host Shahir Massoud tries out recipes from an award-winning cookbook: Chinese-ish by Joanna Hu and Rosheen Kaul! Follow along as Shahir makes fried rice fit for a billionaire, Australian-inspired shrimp toast, comforting chicken soup, and much more!

programme.title: Cook the Books
programme.desc: Chef-host Shahir Massoud tries out recipes from another award-winning cookbook: Pasta Every Day by Meryl Feinstein! Follow along as Shahir demonstrates how to make a variety of pasta shapes, like cavatelli, farfalle, and gnocchi, complete with delicious sauces!

programme.title: One BIG Recipe
programme.desc: When planning a multi-course dinner for six, we all think 'go with the classics' and so does sassy chef-host Kim Lallouz. For her, however, it's Classics... with a Twist! Use the One big Recipe and cook along as Bread and Butter gets a smoke, honey, herb, and black sea salt makeover. Next, Shrimp Cocktail is reinvented with a horseradish kick in her Apricot Ketchup. Then, it's Potato Pave as a standalone course, garnished with tangy tarragon crÃ¨me fraiche and briny salmon roe. Prime Rib is as classic as it comes, but with a sous-vide twist and truffle-spiked Pea and Lardon salad, plan to drop everything so you can twist along! For a sweet ending, dress up dessert from your favourite bakery with a cool trick for making easy lavender ice cream. Dinner is love, and you will love this dinner!

programme.title: One BIG Recipe
programme.desc: Spring is in the air for this multi-course dinner One Big Recipe with engaging Chef Kimberly Lallouz. First up, it's Scallop Crudo, and it's peachy! Literally! Lamb Shanks braised with olives and sun-dried tomatoes are next, as an appetizer ragout, lovingly stuffed into pillowy, perfect mini-Yorkshire Puddings. Next, verdant spears of asparagus appear, topped with creamy, crunchy, soft-cooked egg Sauce Gribiche. Halibut is the main event, pan-roasted steaks are served over king trumpet Dill Pesto Mushrooms and finished with many melty coins of punchy Pink Peppercorn Compound Butter. The entire dinner is laid out in the signature One Big Recipe format, easy to follow, easy to make and guaranteed to impress!

programme.title: Griddle Nation
programme.desc: Comfort and indulgence meet in the griddle’s glow as cheese turns to molten perfection. From timeless classics to inventive creations, these dishes stretch, crisp, and bubble, proving everything tastes better with a little golden melt.

programme.title: Griddle Nation
programme.desc: Spice lovers rejoice as the griddle ignites with bold flavor. From smoky peppers to bright heat and layered seasoning, this exploration celebrates the art of balancing fire and flavor. Home cooks experiment with spice to create dishes that bring warmth and excitement to every bite.

programme.title: Up the Dish
programme.desc: Up the Dish host Micah Trainor tries her hand at an Italian original - Veal Piccata! Lemony, buttery, capERS-y veal melts in the mouth after being pounded supERS-thin and seared to perfection. Poppy Sandler's sideways switch-up is a Caper Almond Crusted Halibut, sizzled and baked until gorgeously golden-brown and flaky, then garnished with chives and pea shoots. Next, Missy Hui goes Greek with her vegetarian Avgolemono, a silky-smooth citrusy soup starring lemon and egg, garnished with orzo and shimeji mushrooms. Tiera Singh ties it all together with her Sumac Veal Sweetbreads with a rough chopped caperberry-olive tapenade - browned veal sweetbreads are sprinkled with tart sumac and laid on a bed of tapenade, then drizzled with a sweet 'n' sour pomegranate molasses vinaigrette.

programme.title: Up the Dish
programme.desc: First up on this episode of Up the Dish is Poppy Sandler's Borscht straight from the Eastern Bloc! A mix of roasted and simmered beets adds complexity to the creamy soup - and don't forget that vivid burgundy hue. Tiera Singh swaps soup for a sandwich with her Beet-Stained Trout Gravlax, pairing the eye-catching seafood with zesty lemon-dill cream cheese and rye pumpernickel bread. Next, Micah Trainor mixes up a vegetarian Roasted Beet Salad paired with crackly Seed Brittle and a simple sweet dressing; it's a crunchy, earthy delight! Missy Hui's three-layer Red Beet Velvet Cake is a true triple threat. Red beet puree gives this cake its vermillion colour - and pairing it with tangy goat cheese icing... well, it just makes sense!

programme.title: Up the Dish
programme.desc: In this episode of Up the Dish, host Micah Trainor takes a stab at Cassoulet with buttery white beans, duck leg confit, and flavourful French sausage - and the result is mouth-wateringly good! The pressure's on, but Tiera Singh doesn't let up with her Chinese Long Bean Stir Fry. Succulent sausage, delicious duck breast, and flavorful vegetables come together for a truly sumptuous dish! For her vegetarian variant, Missy Hui must move things up a notch, but how? Agedashi Tofu! Breaded crispy semi-firm tofu is doused in a dashi, gochujang, and mirin mixture for the ultimate umami mouthful. programme.title: Bonacini's Italy
programme.desc: Renowned Chef Michael Bonacini takes viewers on a culinary tour of the Italian region of Trentino-Alto Adige without leaving his own kitchen.

programme.title: Bonacini's Italy
programme.desc: Charming host Chef Michael Bonacini explores the mountainous region of Basilicata with a feast fit for any celebration. Beginning with an antipasto that caters to the region's southern heat, Michael stuffs crunchy sweet peppers with ripe tomatoes, savoury anchovies, and fresh oregano. Moving on to the primo, Michael cooks up a creamy Vermicelli Frittata. Using leftover pasta, he combines eggs with cheese, anchovies, and chili flakes for extra flavour to make a classic Basilicata dish. Michael cooks up Pollo Alla Potentina, a traditional meal from the Feast of Saint Gerard. This hearty chicken stew is sure to heat up your core, using hot peppers as the base of the meal. For his final dish, Michael combines the rich tastes of pomegranate, walnuts, and dark chocolate to create his unique Sweet Wheat Bowl.

programme.desc: Seafood loving chef Spencer Watts gets casual and formal with monkfish and clam recipes that feature some of his favorite libations - beer and wine.

programme.title: Fish the Dish
programme.desc: Chef Spencer Watts is making a cookbook all about fish. Here he cooks up some swordfish and scallop recipes that feature some of his favorite nuts.

programme.title: Fish the Dish

programme.title: Class tv moda stories
programme.desc: Una copertura speciale e completa sulle fiere di settore (Pitti Immagine, Salone del Mobile, Vicenzaoro, Micam, Mipel, ecc.)

programme.title: Dazzling Blue Lakeshore
programme.desc: Relaxing on the blue shoreline of New Zealand's famous Lake Pukaki; filmed in January 2017 on New Zealand's South Island.

programme.title: Above the Fiji Islands
programme.desc: Featuring aerial views of remote South Pacific islands, accompanied by nature sounds and ambient music by Relax Moods; offering a mix of ground-level and bird's-eye perspectives through smooth, continuous shots, concluding with a real-time sunset over the sea.

programme.title: Sunny Sandy Beach: Dominican Republic
programme.desc: Unwinding by an idyllic stretch of sand where time stands still surrounded by luxurious flora and light navy blue waters.

programme.title: Wairere Falls: New Zealand
programme.desc: The Wairere Falls of New Zealand.

programme.title: Hidden Tropical Paradise 2
programme.desc: Taveuni and Small Outer Islands of Fiji.

programme.title: Hollyford Track: New Zealand
programme.desc: A remote beech forest full of diverse vegetation, waterfalls and rocky streams.

programme.title: Camping by the Lake
programme.desc: Camping out in nature with a relaxing fire next to a quiet lake.

programme.title: Star beach: Philippines
programme.desc: Exploring a small and secluded tropical oasis with soft and white sandy beaches, crystal-clear waters and rugged rock formations.

programme.title: Diamond Beach I: Indonesia
programme.desc: An immersion into turquoise waters and a rocky coastline in Bali, Indonesia.

programme.title: Lush Vineyards: Portugal
programme.desc: The jaw-droppingly beautiful Douro Valley, Portugal's most scenic wine regions.

programme.title: Healing Hawaii: Kauai
programme.desc: A visual and audio journey around the exotic island paradise of Kauai, known as the Garden Isle. 60 different one-minute scenes of nature, slowly transitioning from one to the next.

programme.title: Above the Rocky Mountains
programme.desc: Showcasing one of the world's most famous mountain ranges, Banff National Park, from a higher perspective and is paired with healing music and subtle real-life nature sounds; soaring over places like Lake Louise and more from the Canadian Rockies.

programme.title: Above the Tulip Fields
programme.desc: Tulip fields as seen from a drone perspective in the windmill-filled country of Holland.

programme.title: Mount Cook: New Zealand
programme.desc: Taking in the rugged beauty of snow-capped mountains and calm emerald waters.

programme.title: Tutoko River: New Zealand
programme.desc: Rushing waters through rocky rapids.

programme.title: Underwater Wonders
programme.desc: Highlighting the abundant and varied flora and fauna that dwells underwater in several oceans around the world.

programme.title: Far North Beaches: New Zealand
programme.desc: The tranquil beaches of New Zealand's Far North, complete with small coves.

programme.title: Calming Mountain Waterfall
programme.desc: A fixed-angle one hour long nature scene of the beautiful Tangle Falls in Jasper National Park, paired with real stereo nature sounds shot on location; although it's a static video, there are a lot of dynamic elements.

programme.title: Dawn in Paradise
programme.desc: The magical sounds of a tropical island in the early morning hours just before sunrise.

programme.title: Ruby Beach Sunset

programme.title: Malini Angelica Napoli
programme.desc: Malini Angelica visits Naples, in Italy’s vibrant Campania region. She dives into the city’s beautiful chaos to uncover the heart of Neapolitan culture, sampling delicious street food, meeting friendly locals, and exploring its rich history. With its authenticity and energy, Naples stands out as one of Italy’s most captivating cities.

programme.title: Whats On Special and Unique Festivals
programme.desc: From Malta to Morocco, unique festivals capture Mediterranean creativity. Malta hosts the Valletta Green Festival, Notte Bianca, Malta Air Show, and Gharb International Kite and Wind Festival. Portugal celebrates with Festa das Cruzes, Morocco Dream City Festival dazzles with arts and music, Croatia brings the avant garde MoDem Festival, and Italy hosts the historic Venice Debutante Grand Ball. These festivals showcase the extraordinary, the festive, and the unforgettable.

programme.title: Med Destinations Delos

programme.title: Rick Steves Europe

programme.title: Med Nations 03 Turkey
programme.desc: Turkey, where cultures, continents, and centuries of history all come together. Turkey has a lot of different traditions, flavours, and history that have been shaped by powerful empires and cultural crossroads that have been around for hundreds of years. You can see this from the busy streets of Istanbul to the sunny Mediterranean coast.

programme.title: Mediterranean Yoga Slow Down and Breathe

Set in stunning coastal and natural locations, each episode encourages viewers to reconnect mind and body through gentle movement, breathwork, and mindfulness in a safe and calming space.

programme.desc: Salvador Dalí, the eccentric Mediterranean visionary, turned his life into art and became one of history’s most unforgettable minds.

programme.title: Bronzi di Riace il tesoro ritrovato
programme.desc: Calabria, a land steeped in myths, reveals its secrets through the Riace Bronzes. Restorer Cosimo Schepis shares the story of these ancient treasures, 50 years after their discovery. Actress Swamy Rotolo and her sister Grecia journey through Calabria, uncovering its hidden gems. A captivating exploration of the myths and legacy of Magna Grecia.

programme.desc: The Mediterranean celebrates athleticism. Italy hosts The Torino Royal Marathon and Maratona Reale, while France Roc d Azur brings mountain biking excitement. From road races to endurance challenges, sports unite communities along stunning Mediterranean landscapes.

programme.title: Malini Angelica Lisbon
programme.desc: Malini Angelica explores Lisbon, the vibrant capital of Portugal. Once founded by the Phoenicians and shaped by Romans, Moors, and explorers of the Golden Age, Lisbon’s layered history is reflected in its culture and charm. During her visit, the city comes alive with the Festas Populares de Lisboa — a June celebration of Saint Anthony filled with music, dancing, and barbecued sardines. Malini samples local favorites like grilled sardines, bifanas, and the famous pastel de nata, and experiences the soulful beauty of Fado music. Lisbon’s warmth, flavor, and festive spirit show why it remains one of Europe’s most beloved cities.

programme.title: Dates that made History
programme.desc: 399 BC: The Trial of Socrates dramatises the famous trial of the Greek philosopher Socrates, who is charged with corrupting the youth and impiety. The episode delves into Socrates' defence, his philosophical ideas, and his unwavering commitment to his principles. It explores the tension between individual thought and the societal norms of ancient Athens. Through this trial, the show examines the impact of Socrates' ideas on Western philosophy and his legacy.

programme.title: Malini Angelica Andalucia
programme.desc: Join Angelica Malini on an unforgettable journey through Andalucía, southern Spain’s most colorful and captivating region, as they explore Cordoba and Cadiz. Famous for flamenco, tapas, and stunning Moorish architecture, Andalucía’s rich history comes alive here. In Cordoba, the host marvels at the breathtaking Mezquita, a grand mosque turned cathedral. In Cadiz, an ancient Phoenician city, they soak in sweeping Atlantic views and a corniche with hints of North Africa. This episode immerses viewers in the culture, history, and vibrant spirit of one of Spain’s most enchanting regions.

programme.title: Med Whats On Extraordinary Events
programme.desc: Spring is transforming the Mediterranean into a massive stage for high-tech art and spectacular outdoor events. We begin at the Ta Qali National Park for the Bloom Festival, where wellness and music create a vibrant sanctuary in the heart of Malta. From there, we head into the tunnels and forts of Valletta and Birgu for the Malta Biennale 2026 to discover contemporary installations hidden inside historic sites rarely seen by the public. Our journey takes us to the cliffs of Gozo as giant creative designs fill the sky for the International Kite and Wind Festival before we land in Venice to witness the world class creativity of the 61st International Art Exhibition. This is a look at a region in the middle of a stunning cultural rebirth.

programme.title: Azzurro Storie di mare: Alla scoperta di Ischia, isola verde
programme.desc: A journey along Italy’s coast, from Ponza to Calabria, exploring its natural beauty and the people who protect it. Fishermen, divers, and activists share their passion for the sea and their fight against pollution.

programme.title: Volcano Stories
programme.desc: Each of its tremors is constantly monitored : the Vesuvius is one of the world’s most famous and dangerous volcanoes. At its feet lies the city of Naples, the third largest city in Italy, and its 970,000 inhabitants.

programme.title: Med Destinations Ios
programme.desc: Discover the lively spirit and scenic beauty of Ios on Med Destinations. From crystal-clear beaches to winding streets and sunset views, Ios combines vibrant island life with the tranquility of the Aegean Sea.

programme.title: Great Myths the Iliad
programme.desc: Agamemnon has kidnapped the beautiful Chryseis and her enslavement angers the Gods. He agrees to return her to her father, but only if Achilles gives him his own enslaved concubine, Briseis. Forced to hand her over, Achilles is furious and says he won't fight for Agamemnon until he gets his concubine back.

programme.title: Concerto - OSN Marc Albrecht E Simone Lamsma Torino
programme.desc: In this 2022 concert in Turin, Marc Albrecht conducts the Orchestra Sinfonica Nazionale della Rai, with Simone Lamsma as the violin soloist. The programme includes Shostakovich's Violin Concerto No. 1, showcasing Lamsma's technical brilliance and emotional depth, and Franck's Symphony in D minor, celebrated for its rich harmonies and cyclical structure. The performance highlights a powerful blend of passion and precision.

programme.title: Sinj
programme.desc: Sinj blends courage, tradition, and warm hospitality—reflected even in its food. Edo Crljen welcomed us with a bold serving of tripes, while Ankica Vlastelica prepared the town’s pride: arambaši, Sinj’s iconic stuffed cabbage rolls you simply can’t leave without tasting.

programme.title: Med Destination - Dubrovnik
programme.desc: WITH VO

programme.title: Malini Angelica Barcelona
programme.desc: Barcelona, Spain, is a city close to Malini's heart. Returning after her student years, she retraces old steps to see how the city has changed. Barcelona, one of Europe’s most visited cities, still holds centuries-old charm. Malina's old haunts include El Gòtic, Sant Antoni, Passeig de Gràcia, and Barceloneta. visits Gaudí’s iconic Casa La Pedrera, and discovers the new wave of creative tapas restaurants in El Raval, a once run-down quarter now bursting with energy.

programme.title: Rick Steves Europe
programme.desc: The true soul of Greece lies in the Peloponnese, a rugged peninsula where dramatic mountains have witnessed the rise and fall of civilizations for centuries.

programme.title: Concerto - OSN Marc Albrecht E Simone Lamsma Torino
programme.desc: In this 2022 concert in Turin, Marc Albrecht conducts the Orchestra Sinfonica Nazionale della Rai, with Simone Lamsma as the violin soloist. The programme includes Shostakovich's Violin Concerto No. 1, showcasing Lamsma's technical brilliance and emotional depth, and Franck's Symphony in D minor, celebrated for its rich harmonies and cyclical structure. The performance highlights a powerful blend of passion and precision.

programme.title: Med Whats On Extraordinary Events
programme.desc: Spring is transforming the Mediterranean into a massive stage for high-tech art and spectacular outdoor events. We begin at the Ta Qali National Park for the Bloom Festival, where wellness and music create a vibrant sanctuary in the heart of Malta. From there, we head into the tunnels and forts of Valletta and Birgu for the Malta Biennale 2026 to discover contemporary installations hidden inside historic sites rarely seen by the public. Our journey takes us to the cliffs of Gozo as giant creative designs fill the sky for the International Kite and Wind Festival before we land in Venice to witness the world class creativity of the 61st International Art Exhibition. This is a look at a region in the middle of a stunning cultural rebirth.

programme.title: MED Destination VENICE
programme.desc: WITH VO

programme.title: Med Destination - Dubrovnik
programme.desc: WITH VO

programme.title: MED Destination ANTIPAROS
programme.desc: WITH VO

programme.title: Mediterranean Yoga Let go of tension
programme.desc: Mediterranean Yoga is a series that blends Embodiment Yoga with the peaceful beauty of the Mediterranean. Set in stunning coastal and natural locations, each episode encourages viewers to reconnect mind and body through gentle movement, breathwork, and mindfulness in a safe and calming space.

programme.title: Memoirs July 15
programme.desc: Memoirs July 15

programme.title: Whats On Music Festivals All Over the Med
programme.desc: The Mediterranean comes alive to the sound of music. From Malta high energy Rock the South and sophisticated Malta Jazz Festival, to Italy cutting edge Limbo Festival, Spain forward thinking Sonar Festival, and Turkey classical and contemporary Istanbul Music Festival, every corner of the region vibrates with rhythm, passion, and creativity.

programme.title: Med Minds 06 Jean de Valette
programme.desc: Jean Parisot de Valette, the steadfast Grand Master of Malta, led the Knights with courage, defended his island, and saved Europe as we know it.

programme.title: Med Nations 01 Italy
programme.desc: The heart of Italy! A place where old empires, the brilliance of the Renaissance, and modern passion all come together. Italy shows itself through its traditions, flavours, and lively way of life. From the timeless beauty of Rome to the artistic heart of Florence and the vibrant energy of Naples, Italy is alive with life. It's a journey through a country that has had a profound impact on art, cuisine, and culture throughout the Mediterranean and beyond.

programme.title: MED Destination ANTIPAROS
programme.desc: WITH VO

programme.title: Med Destinations Lake Bled
programme.desc: Step into the fairytale beauty of Lake Bled, Slovenia, on Med Destinations. Crystal-clear waters, a picturesque island church, and lush Alpine surroundings make it a serene Mediterranean escape.

programme.title: Outdoor and Nature Ep 5
programme.desc: OUTDOOR & Nature, a weekly program that highlights sports, outdoor activities, and the breathtaking scenery of Cyprus, will introduce you to the great outdoors. Each episode highlights government initiatives, community organisations, and volunteer efforts to preserve Cyprus's natural heritage while examining nature, wildlife, and conservation. Take a trip of inspiration, exploration, and adventure with hosts Panicos Pilalis and Aristo Aristeidou. Listen and get motivated to go on your own outdoor adventure in Cyprus!

programme.title: Med Destinations Ancona
programme.desc: Explore Ancona, a bustling port city on the Adriatic, with Med Destinations. Discover its historic landmarks, scenic waterfronts, and the unique blend of maritime tradition and modern Italian life.

programme.title: Into the Med Gozo Island of Traditions
programme.desc: Born on the sunny island of Gozo, Susannah always had a connection to the Mediterranean. After moving to the Netherlands as a child, she is returning to the Mediterranean to rediscover her roots. Starting with the raw beauty of her home island, she invites you to explore the Mediterranean through a lens of discovery and belonging.

programme.title: Med Nations 01 Italy
programme.desc: The heart of Italy! A place where old empires, the brilliance of the Renaissance, and modern passion all come together. Italy shows itself through its traditions, flavours, and lively way of life. From the timeless beauty of Rome to the artistic heart of Florence and the vibrant energy of Naples, Italy is alive with life. It's a journey through a country that has had a profound impact on art, cuisine, and culture throughout the Mediterranean and beyond.

programme.title: Split (Firule)
programme.desc: In Split’s Firule neighborhood—home to Croatia’s tennis greats—we discovered more than just sport. Renowned chef Hrvoje Zirojević welcomed us with a taste of tradition: pivac na tingul, a classic Dalmatian stew made with tender young rooster.

programme.title: World Spas
programme.desc: Jamie Srubis tries some of the signature spa treatments of the Shangri La Resort at Kota Kinabalu in Sabah, Malaysia.

programme.title: Unplanned Trip
programme.desc: On a visit to Hawai, the land of the Mishmi tribe, Roshni meets a tribe member who teaches her how to catch fish. Later that day, she stays at the house of a Mishmi family, and they treat her to a local drink that makes her shed her inhibitions and open up to them in no time.

programme.title: Viajar Peru
programme.desc: In the last leg of her journey, Milagros goes to Trujillo, where she introduces the flavors of the northern region of Peru and also Marinera – the sensuous national dance. But the highlight in Trujillo are the forgotten sites like Chan Chan and Huaca del Moche, where secrets from Peru’s pre-Incan past begin to unfold.

programme.title: 10 Days Scotland
programme.desc: A modern European oil city built on the back of a rich maritime heritage, Aberdeen is the energetic kingpin of northeast Scotland. While the boom times may come and go, the dynamic coastal metropolis never ceases to thrill with its free museums, outdoor adventures and natural attractions right on the city’s doorstep.

programme.title: Xplore Austria
programme.desc: Elisha is on a trip to Salzburg, the birthplace of Mozart, whose legacy still echoes throughout Salzburg’s Baroque residences, auditoriums and dining halls. Paying her respects at the renowned composer’s memorial, and attending a musical based on the story of the Von Trapp family, she finds herself starstruck by the rich musical heritage of this charming city.

programme.title: City Breaks
programme.desc: When Yogita’s business plans in Bengaluru unravel, she ends up with 48 hours of leisure and no idea what to do in the city. Enter Shruti – her friend, and a diehard fan of the city she calls home. From quiet mornings in one of Bengaluru’s many parks to lively evenings at one of its many breweries, from rustic Kannada feasts to thrilling adventures – Shruti wants Yogita to experience it all. But are two days enough for her to make Yogita fall in love with the city that has captured her heart?

programme.title: Quest
programme.desc: While learning to prepare a Naga pork delicacy, Alex gets an understanding of the different dishes and cooking styles of Nagaland’s tribes.

programme.title: Unplanned Trip
programme.desc: Crisp mountain breezes, lush greenery and gurgling streams – Roing is a town that entices Roshni to explore it on foot. A visit to the local market leads to her sampling an infamous chilli that has reduced seasoned foodies to tears. Later on, she takes a tour of the “Orange Bowl of Arunachal Pradesh”.

programme.title: Great World Hotels
programme.desc: Moeen, a travel enthusiast, goes for a short getaway to the Closenberg Hotel in Galle, an 18th century Dutch walled town in Sri Lanka with grand colonial houses and beautiful beaches. At the resort, he chances upon a traditional Sri Lankan wedding, which offers him a glimpse of the country’s traditions and cuisine.

programme.title: 10 Days Scotland
programme.desc: Since medieval times Aberdeenshire and its northwestern neighbour Moray have been the richest and most fertile regions of the Highlands. Aberdeenshire is famed for its Aberdeen Angus beef cattle, its many fine castles and the prosperous 'granite city' of Aberdeen.

programme.title: Xplore Austria
programme.desc: On a trip to Innsbruck, Elisha is instantly charmed by the Old Town, where she feasts her eyes on the city’s iconic attractions. From the famous ‘Golden Roof’ to the Imperial Church, the monuments she visits reveal to her stories of a 15th century emperor, under whose reign the city had reached its zenith.

programme.title: World Spas
programme.desc: At Maya Ubud Spa Resort, Jamie Srubis tries different spa treatments that induce the same tranquility which Bali does.

programme.title: Quest
programme.desc: From traditional smoked pork in Nagaland to frog meat in Shillong, Alex finds out the secrets of tribal cuisine in Nagaland.

programme.title: Unplanned Trip
programme.desc: Did you know one of the world’s most expensive varieties of tea is produced in Arunachal Pradesh? Roshni attends a tea festival at the Donyi Polo Tea Estate in Pasighat, where she gets a chance to savour some of the most premium varieties of tea produced in the state.

programme.title: Great World Hotels
programme.desc: From swimming in the ocean to relishing a traditional Maldivian lobster curry, Kim experiences the true meaning of joie de vivre at Reethi Rah.

programme.title: 10 Days Scotland
programme.desc: In the enchanting landscapes of Inverness, Robbie’s journey takes a whimsical turn: from eccentric to downright magical.

programme.title: Xplore Oman
programme.desc: From the magnificent Sultan Qaboos Grand Mosque to the enchanting Muttrah Souq, Elisha experiences the classic Arabic charms of Oman, in Muscat.

programme.title: World Spas
programme.desc: Shirodhara is a traditional Ayurvedic oil drip treatment that popular world traveler, Jamie Srubis tries at the Mines Wellness Resort in Malaysia. Following this, she tries other treatments such as Urut Melayu, a full body massage which derives its technique from a traditional Malay martial art, and Acupuncture and Moxibustion, the two oldest treatments of Traditional Chinese Medicine.

programme.title: Quest
programme.desc: While cooking the local fish of the Jaintia region of Meghalaya, Alex also gets acquainted with the culinary heritage of Sylhet in Bangladesh.

programme.title: Xplore Oman
programme.desc: From the limestone formations of Wadi Bani Khaled to the ocean of dunes at Wahiba Sands, Elisha gets a taste of Oman’s natural heritage.

programme.title: Unplanned Trip
programme.desc: Roshni goes to the adventure hub of Arunachal Pradesh – Mechuka. Getting a bird’s eye view of the mountains and ziplining across breathtaking vistas gives her an intense adrenaline rush that instantly makes her besotted with the town.

programme.title: Great World Hotels
programme.desc: Located in the North Malé Atoll of Maldives, the Reethi Rah resort is the perfect combination of island life mixed with world-class luxury.

programme.title: World Spas
programme.desc: Jamie Srubis tries some of the signature spa treatments of the Shangri La Resort at Kota Kinabalu in Sabah, Malaysia.

programme.title: 10 Days Scotland
programme.desc: The grand finale of Robbie’s wild homecoming unfolds amidst the rugged beauty of the Isle of Skye.

programme.desc: While cooking the local fish of the Jaintia region of Meghalaya, Alex also gets acquainted with the culinary heritage of Sylhet in Bangladesh.

programme.desc: From the limestone formations of Wadi Bani Khaled to the ocean of dunes at Wahiba Sands, Elisha gets a taste of Oman’s natural heritage.

programme.desc: Located in the North Malé Atoll of Maldives, the Reethi Rah resort is the perfect combination of island life mixed with world-class luxury.

programme.desc: Jamie Srubis tries some of the signature spa treatments of the Shangri La Resort at Kota Kinabalu in Sabah, Malaysia.

programme.title: 10 Days Scotland
programme.desc: The grand finale of Robbie’s wild homecoming unfolds amidst the rugged beauty of the Isle of Skye.

programme.title: 10 Days Scotland
programme.desc: Robbie’s adventures in Perth kick off with him immersing himself in the historic grandeur of the Scone Castle, where a mysterious stone tells tales of Scottish monarchs. But the real thunder of the Highlands awaits at the Braemar Games gathering, where tartan meets brawn, and Robbie learns that embracing his Scottish side involves more than just kilts and castles.

programme.title: Backpack Serbia
programme.desc: Heather explores Novi Sad, the financial and industrial hub of Serbia.Starting with canoeing on Danube and later exploring the city and its landmarks.

programme.title: Boarding Pass
programme.desc: Emeline Nsingi Nkosi goes to Santa Village at Rovaniemi, where she reads some of the letters that children from around the world write to him.

programme.title: Xplore Bulgaria
programme.desc: Luxurious hotels, thrilling water parks, inexpensive food and almost 300 days of bright sunny weather make Bulgaria’s biggest seaside resort, Sunny Beach popular for budget beach getaways. Catriona, however, stumbles onto the historic town of Nessebar, which is over 3000 years old, and realizes that there is more to the place than meets the eye. She goes on to visit the Castra Rubra winery, where she finds out about the history of Viticulture in Bulgaria and the process of wine making.

programme.title: City Breaks
programme.desc: Benafsha embarks on a whirlwind adventure in Ahmedabad. With just 48 hours in the city, she is determined to make the most of it, but how?

programme.title: Food Fact Fun
programme.desc: From exploring a vintage car museum to scaling a 5 meter ice-wall and sampling a rich bread cookie, Watch Alex exploring The Hague, also known as the “International city of peace”.

programme.title: 10 Days Scotland
programme.desc: A modern European oil city built on the back of a rich maritime heritage, Aberdeen is the energetic kingpin of northeast Scotland. While the boom times may come and go, the dynamic coastal metropolis never ceases to thrill with its free museums, outdoor adventures and natural attractions right on the city’s doorstep.

programme.title: Backpack Serbia
programme.desc: Heather explores Požarevac and visits the ancient town of Viminacium. Later visits National Museum, concluding with Majdanpek’s Rajkova Pecina Caves.

programme.title: Boarding Pass
programme.desc: Emeline Nsingi Nkosi goes to Rovaniemi, where she visits a husky farm and finds out about about breeding and raising huskies.

programme.title: Xplore Bulgaria
programme.desc: For the people of Sofia, preserving its heritage is sacrosanct, and this can be seen in the way the city's infrastructure is built around its archeological treasures. Catriona realizes this when she roams around the cobblestone streets of the city. With every monument she visits, she finds herself transported to a different era, right from Perperikon fort, which narrate stories of the Byzantine empire, to Boyana church, which is home to some of the earliest examples of medieval artwork.

programme.title: Great World Hotels
programme.desc: From lazing in hammocks by the beach to sipping drinks on a private yacht, Kim realizes that Maldives is the perfect destination to unwind in style.

programme.title: Food Fact Fun
programme.desc: From trying dishes like Stroopwafel and Palat Stoofvlees to visiting the Erasmus bridge, cube houses and wall painting on the streets, Alex explores Rotterdam.

programme.title: 10 Days Scotland
programme.desc: Since medieval times Aberdeenshire and its northwestern neighbour Moray have been the richest and most fertile regions of the Highlands. Aberdeenshire is famed for its Aberdeen Angus beef cattle, its many fine castles and the prosperous 'granite city' of Aberdeen.

programme.title: Backpack Serbia
programme.desc: Explore Majdanpek with our host as the city brings the viewers closer to the treasures strewn across the Serbian countryside.

programme.title: Boarding Pass
programme.desc: Levi is Finland's largest and fastest-growing ski destination. Emeline is here, to find out the exciting activities that Levi offers, in summer.

programme.title: Xplore Bhutan
programme.desc: With snow-capped peaks rising over primeval forests and traditional villages, Paro is like an Oriental Switzerland. On a visit to Paro, Radhika explores famous monasteries such as the Rinpung Dzong and Takstang Monastery, and learns the history and legends associated with them. Along the way, she finds out the secrets to the quintessential Bhutanese peace and contentment.

programme.title: Great World Hotels
programme.desc: An invigorating spa treatment, a cooking lesson, a snorkeling session – Kim has experiences in the Maldives that leave her completely rejuvenated.

programme.title: Food Fact Fun
programme.desc: From trying Orange zest beer and traditional Dutch dishes to exploring St.Martin's Cathedral, Dom Tower and enjoying go karting and stand up paddle boat ride, watch Alex enjoy his trip in Utrecht.

programme.title: 10 Days Scotland
programme.desc: In the enchanting landscapes of Inverness, Robbie’s journey takes a whimsical turn: from eccentric to downright magical.

programme.title: Backpack Serbia
programme.desc: Heather explores Nis,one of the oldest cities in Europe.She visits a concentration camp and later paraglides to end by watching a musical performance.

programme.title: Boarding Pass
programme.desc: Emeline goes to Ruka-Kuusamo, where she tries river rafting. She also meets a reindeer herder,who tells her about the unique ways of herding reindeer.

programme.title: Xplore Bhutan
programme.desc: Actress and travel enthusiast, Radhika Bangia explores the enigma that is Bhutan, when she visits Thimpu, the capital city with no traffic lights and no skyscrapers invading the skyline. Here, she checks out significant sights such as the Tibetan-style National Memorial Chorten, the National Library and the Folk Heritage Museum in order to understand more about the beliefs, history, architecture and cuisine of Bhutan.

programme.title: World Heritage
programme.desc: Explore the ancient heritage sites of Abu Simbel, the Philae complex of Nubia and the Hatshepsut Temple to unravel the mystical stories behind the ancient temples of Egypt.

programme.title: Food Fact Fun
programme.desc: Alex tries traditional Dutch food in Leiden, learns about Dutch history at Prinsenhof Museum, visits a Gothic church, a local cheese market and Delftware factory at Gouda.

programme.title: 10 Days Scotland
programme.desc: The grand finale of Robbie’s wild homecoming unfolds amidst the rugged beauty of the Isle of Skye.

programme.title: Backpack Serbia
programme.desc: Continue exploring Serbia with Heather as she visits the Manasija monastery, Resavska caves, ending with a joyride onboard Eight of Sargan rail line.

programme.title: With Love From Mauritius
programme.desc: Krisann and Nathan’s vacation in Mauritius begins in Le Morne, where an unexpected adventure awaits the latter.

programme.title: Xplore Bhutan
programme.desc: Known as Druk Yul or the “Land of the Thunder Dragon”, Bhutan is the country of mountains, monasteries and mystery. Radhika Bangia finds this to be true when she visits Punakha, the former capital of Bhutan, where she marvels at the architecture of Punakha Dzong and gets to know the story behind the two rivers, Mo Chhu and Po Chhu. She also explores the sacred temples and Mebar Tsho lake of Bumthang, the religious heart of Bhutan, and gets to know the local legends.

programme.title: World Spas
programme.desc: Jamie Srubis tries out spa treatments, which are inspired by the Dead Sea and the desert surroundings at Hotel Kempinski Ishtar in Jordan.

programme.title: Food Fact Fun
programme.desc: After Burj Khalifa, Alex has a traditional Emirati platter, visits the gold market and skiing rink, and goes for a camel safari and camping.

programme.title: City break
programme.desc: Nestled in the mighty Alps, tour the Austrian city of Innsbruck with Sabrina and explore a town that’s alpine-urban yet traditional in nature.

programme.title: Backpack Serbia
programme.desc: In her final leg of journey, Heather visits the Uvac canyon, tries locally prepared pie, ending with Kopaonik National Park and Jelovarnik waterfalls.

programme.title: With Love From Mauritius
programme.desc: On an island-hopping adventure in Mauritius, Krisann and Nathan find out that time works differently on these islands.

programme.title: XP Guide
programme.desc: A homely place where the aroma of freshly baked bread greets you as people smoothly glide along on their bicycles. Welcome to Copenhagen.

programme.title: Unwind
programme.desc: Indeya explores the Cinnamon plantations, tries a local dish and witnesses a traditional dance performance.

programme.title: Food Fact Fun
programme.desc: Alex has varied experiences in Dubai like trying Lebanese cheese bread, visiting a coffee museum, going to IMG Worlds of Adventure and more.

programme.title: Food Fact Fun
programme.desc: In Amsterdam, Alex tries Bitterballen, cannabis cake and visits Heineken Experience brewery. He explores Zaanse Schans village with windmills and Coster Diamonds, the oldest diamond polishing factory.

programme.title: 10 Days Scotland
programme.desc: Robbie’s journey to reconnect with his Scottish roots begins in Edinburgh, the stunning capital city perched on extinct volcanoes. Navigating the twists of the Royal Mile, strutting around the majestic Edinburgh castle and learning to sip scotch like a true Scotsman, he feels a tad closer to his heritage. The real fun kicks in, however, when he stumbles onto a rather flamboyant way to unleash his inner Highlander.

programme.title: Xplore Bulgaria
programme.desc: Like Rome, Plovdiv straddles seven hills; but as Europe’s oldest continuously inhabited city, it’s far more ancient. As Catriona takes a tour of the city to see its iconic attractions such as a Roman Amphitheatre, which was once the setting for gladiator fights, and the striking Baroque Saints Constantine and Helena Church, she begins to see how seamlessly modern life blends with the age-old ruins. Along the way, she realizes why Plovdiv is called the ‘Eternal City’ of Bulgaria.

programme.title: XP Guide
programme.desc: Stockholm, the capital and largest city of Sweden, was built on 14 Islands. This place is also sometimes referred as Venice of the north.

programme.title: City Breaks
programme.desc: This exhilarating city of castles, beer and segway rides can take you on a break that will truly leave you catching your breath.

programme.desc: Visit Belgrade, with Heather as she explores the city, its architecture and learns about its past by visiting historical monuments.

programme.title: Change your Destiny
programme.desc: Continuano le audizioni di Antonio Fini e dei suoi giudici per selezionare i migliori danzatori da invitare al prestigioso festival di danza in NEW YORK. I ragazzi affrontano le tre sfide previste: portare il loro pezzo migliore, la contemporary battle e la lezione di Hip hop con Mirco Giordano e Marcello Gargarelli.

programme.title: Il CIRCOLO DEL BUONGUSTAI Con Fabio Campoli
programme.desc: Benvenuti a questo nuovo episodio del Circolo dei Buongustai! Cosa bolle in pentola? Oggi il nostro chef Fabio Campoli ci delizierà con tre ricette speciali: portafoglio di squacquerone, ciliegie e mortadella, riso rosso integrale con pollo e peperoni, diplomatico alle rose. Buona visione a tutti!

programme.title: Il CIRCOLO DEL BUONGUSTAI Con Fabio Campoli
programme.desc: Benvenuti a questo nuovo episodio del Circolo dei Buongustai! Cosa bolle in pentola? Oggi il nostro chef Fabio Campoli ci delizierà con tre ricette speciali: rotolo di riso, prosciutto e melone, risotto alla rucola con kiwi e vongole, e budino di riso. Buona visione a tutti!

programme.title: My Talented Friends Season 1
programme.desc: Ornella Fado introuce il suo amico Geniale Marco Napoli, attore e regista televisivo e cinematografico. Il documentario di oggi vede come protagonista una giovane artista catanese Roberta Finocchiaro "Save lives with the Rhytm.

programme.title: My Talented Friends Season 1
programme.desc: Ornella Fado presenta il lavoro artistico della sua amica Geniale,Paola Sinisgalli fondatrice della "Creative Shakes" il lavoro artistico di oggi e` un corto che vede Paola nei panni di regista.

programme.title: My Talented Friends Season 1
programme.desc: Ornella Fado introuce il suo amico Geniale Marco Napoli, attore e regista televisivo e cinematografico. Il documentario di oggi vede come protagonista una giovane artista catanese Roberta Finocchiaro "Save lives with the Rhytm.

programme.title: Eccellenze Italiane con Piero Muscari intervista a Heinz Beck
programme.desc: Pietro Muscari intervista Lorenzo Suraci, Presidente di RTL 102.5. Lorenzo rivela come ha iniziato a lavorare con celebrità come Maurizio Costanzo e Bruno Vespa e quali sono i progetti futuri per la prima radio in Italia.

programme.title: Eccellenze Italiane con Piero Muscari intervista a Heinz Beck
programme.desc: Pietro Muscari intervista Enrico Vaime, autore e conduttore radio e TV. Con Enrico si parla di radio, tv, libri, ma anche di amore e dispiaceri che hanno formato la sua carriera.

programme.title: Brindiamo!
programme.desc: Questa puntata fa parte della serie vintage. Ornella Fado visita una delle più belle scuole di cucine upstate in New York la "CIA" Culinary Institute of America, dove visita il ristorante "Caterina dei Medici", e con lo chef Scapin preprano alcune ricette del menù della scuola. (2007)

programme.title: Brindiamo!
programme.desc: Questa puntata fa parte della serie vintage. Ornella Fado visita Raffaele Trovato, direttore dell'IFSE, a Piobesi Torinese, oltre a visitare la bellissima scuola, Ornella prepara delle ricette con l'istruttore del centro.(2009)

programme.title: Brindiamo!
programme.desc: Questa puntata fa parte della serie vintage. Ornella Fado visita il ristorante "Il Tartufo" a Manayunk, poco distante da Filadelfia, dove incontra Al Del Bello, per preparare alcune ricette tipiche del ristorante. In questa puntata Ornella indossa i guanti da boxer per correre come Rocky Balboa sulla scalinata del museo d'arte di Filadelfia. (2007)

programme.title: Brindiamo!
programme.desc: Questa puntata fa parte della serie vintage. Ornella Fado visita Al Del Bello al suo ristorante "Il Portico", uno dei ristoranti più raffinati di Filadelfia. Al racconta la sua storia di gratidudine nei confronti della famiglia Lattanzi proprietari di ristoratori italiani a New York. (2006)

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: Ornella Fado si trova nel laboratorio di Urbani Truffle a New York ed invita i suoi amici chef per preparare insieme delle ricette da gustare durante le festività natalizie. In questa puntata Chef Pino Coladonato, Chef Marcello Russodivito e la giovane pizzaiola Giorgia Caporuscio. In questa edizione di Brindiamo! in my Kitchen ospite speciale la soprano Cristina Fontanelli e John La Barbera, oltre a vari ospiti del mondo dello spettacolo, e della moda. (2016)

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: Ornella Fado si trova nel laboratorio di Urbani Truffle a New York per la serconda parte di Brindiamo! in my kitchen. I suoi amici chef in questa puntata son lo chef Raffaele Ronca, lo Chef Marco Chirico e il giovane pizzaiolo Ettore Toches. In questa edizione di Brindiamo! in my Kitchen la musica è dal vivo con la soprano Cristina Fontanelli ed il compositore Jhon La Barbera, tra gli ospiti di questa puntata il Fashion designer Domenico Vacca. (2016)

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: Ornella Fado visita cinque amici ristoratori durante le feste Natalizie, prima tappa" Angelina's" a Staten Island, poi e` la volta di "Marco Polo" e "Luzzo" a Brooklyn. A Manhattan nell'Upper East side, Ornella visita "83 e mezza". Ultima tappa per ujn buon gelato,"Dolce Gelateria" nel Village. (2013)

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: Ornella Fado continua il suo giro per incontrare il suoi amici ristoratori durante le festività di capodanno. Visitando a midtown Chef Pino alla "La Masseria", chef Stefano e Franco a " Viceversa", a "PizzArte" incontra il pizzaiolo Roberto ed il proprietario Dario, a Queens visita "Ornella Trattoria" dove incontra Vincenzo e "Trattoria l'Incontro"dove incontra lo chef e proprietario Rocco. (2013/2014)

programme.title: Brindiamo!
programme.desc: La puntata di oggi è ricca di contenuti. Ornella Fado incontra Cristina Carrani, esperta in materia immobiliare che aiuterà Ornella a scoprire l'Isola di Capri. Dalla moda alla storia, al cibo e all'arte, Brindiamo! Edizione speciale Capri è l'episodio perfetto per festeggiare i 18 anni di Brindiamo! su NYC LIFE.

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: Ornella Fado si trova nel laboratorio di Urbani Truffle a New York ed invita i suoi amici chef per preparare insieme delle ricette da gustare durante le festività natalizie. In questa puntata Chef Pino Coladonato, Chef Marcello Russodivito e la giovane pizzaiola Giorgia Caporuscio. In questa edizione di Brindiamo! in my Kitchen ospite speciale la soprano Cristina Fontanelli e John La Barbera, oltre a vari ospiti del mondo dello spettacolo, e della moda. (2016)

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: Ornella Fado si trova nel laboratorio di Urbani Truffle a New York per la serconda parte di Brindiamo! in my kitchen. I suoi amici chef in questa puntata son lo chef Raffaele Ronca, lo Chef Marco Chirico e il giovane pizzaiolo Ettore Toches. In questa edizione di Brindiamo! in my Kitchen la musica è dal vivo con la soprano Cristina Fontanelli ed il compositore Jhon La Barbera, tra gli ospiti di questa puntata il Fashion designer Domenico Vacca. (2016)

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: Ornella Fado visita il ristorante "Lincoln Square Steak House", a due passi da Lincoln Center. In questa puntata Ornella prepara alcune ricette con il Principe Emanule Filiberto di Savoia, gia` ospite dell'evento di Carnevale di alcuni giorni prima, lo chef Marcello Russodivito insegna al Principe come preprare una succulenta bistecca e prepara un piatto dedicato alla Regina Margherita. Questa puntata fa parte della stagione speciale "Brindiamo! International Cuisine". (2016)

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: Ornella Fado visita il ristorante di "Angelina's" a Staten Island per preparare alcune ricette con Vincent Malerba proprietario del ristorante, ospite di eccezione la cantante Giada Valenti che oltre a preparare una ricetta con Vincent racconta ad Ornella il suo sogno americano. (2016)

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: Ornella Fado con lo chef Raffaele Ronca, visitano il famoso ristorante giapponese "Sushi Nakasawa", oltre a preprare sushi con Chef Nakasawa, lo chef Raffaele Ronca prepara un piatto a quattro mani con Daisuke Nakasawa e poi un sei mani con Alessandro Borgognone, co- proprietario del ristorante, questa puntata fa parte della stagione speciale "Brindiamo! International Cuisine". (2016)

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: In questo epsiodio di Brindiamo! Ornella Fado fa un viaggio dei suoi momenti televisivi. Intervista il famoso chef Joy Campanaro e con chef Ronca preparano gli spaghetti alla chitarra, fa visita a Vincenzo Cutrone allo stabilmento di Brooklyn, "Octopus Garden", presenta una competizione per la regione Basilicata al ristorante Marco Polo, e visita lo chef Marcello Russodivito che festeggia 30 anni del ristorante e conduce un concerto a Suffern NY. (2016)

programme.title: Change your Destiny
programme.desc: Nella prima puntata Antonio Fini ed i suoi giudici incontrano il primo gruppo di ballerini.Per essere selezionati per il prestigioso festival di danza in NEW YORK devono affrontare 3 sfide: presentare il loro pezzo migliore, la contemporary battle e la lezione di danza "tecnica Graham" con il maestro Antonio Fini.

programme.title: Change your Destiny
programme.desc: Continuano le audizioni di Antonio Fini e dei suoi giudici per selezionare i migliori danzatori da invitare al prestigioso festival di danza in NEW YORK. I ragazzi affrontano le tre sfide previste: portare il loro pezzo migliore, la contemporary battle e la lezione di Hip hop con Mirco Giordano e Marcello Gargarelli.

programme.title: Change your Destiny
programme.desc: Terza ed ultima puntata della serie. Antonio Fini ed i suoi giudici sono pronti: chi sceglieranno per volare a NEW YORK per il prestigioso festival della danza? Ancora una volta i ragazzi devono convincere l'importante giuria con il loro pezzo migliore, la contemporary battle e la lezione di danza "tecnica Parson" con la maestra Abby Silva.

programme.title: Brindiamo!
programme.desc: Questa puntata fa parte della serie vintage. Ornella Fado incontra chef Rocco Sacramone al ristorante Trattoria "l'Incontro", Chef Rocco, condivide alcune delle ricette più amate dai suoi clienti incluso una dolcissima pizza alla nutella. (2008)

programme.title: Brindiamo!
programme.desc: Questa puntata fa parte della serie vintage. Ornella Fado incontra un vero gentiluomo della ristorazione italiana a Filadelfia, Giuseppe Giuliani, patron de "La Buca". Giuseppe oltre a preparare alcune ricette toscane, racconta l'avventura dell'apertura atipica e fuori programma del ristorante per ospitare il Maestro Luciano Pavarotti in tour a Filadelfia.(2006)

programme.title: Brindiamo!
programme.desc: Questa puntata fa parte della serie vintage. Ornella Fado visita una delle pizzerie piu` autentiche di New York,con uno dei 5 forni a legna più antichi della città`. "Luzzo" Pizzeria si trova a due passi dal quartiere trendy, il Village. Con Michele Iuliano, pizzaiolo napoletano di grande esperienza, Ornella prepara delle deliziose pizze, alcune tradizionali come la classica margherita ed alcune con un twist. Ospite della puntata il giovane talento Italiano il tenore Luciamo La Monarca.(2008)

programme.title: Brindiamo!

Dalla moda alla storia, al cibo e all'arte, Brindiamo! Edizione speciale Capri è l'episodio perfetto per festeggiare i 18 anni di Brindiamo! su NYC LIFE.

programme.title: Brindiamo!
programme.desc: Questa puntata fa parte della serie vintage. Ornella Fado visita il ristorante toscano "Pepolino" dove prepara delle saporite ricette con lo chef Patrizio, per chiudere in "Dolcezza e Bellezza" Enzo prepara una delle cheese cake piu` famose di New York.(2007).

programme.title: Brindiamo!
programme.desc: Questa puntata fa parte della serie vintage. Ornella Fado visita uno dei ristoranti più eleganti di Manhattan a due passi da Central Park il "San Domenico ristorante". Nella cucina del ristorante Ornella assiste Odette Fada nella preparazione dei classici piatti Italiani. Ospite della puntata la giovane stilista Nicole Romano. Ornella Fado indossa alcune creazioni della sua collezione. (2007)

programme.title: Brindiamo!
programme.desc: Questa puntata fa parte della serie vintage. Ornella Fado visita uno dei ristoranti più belli d'Europa, "Don Alfonso 1890" dove incontra la famiglia Iaccarino. Oltre a preparare le ricette nella bellissima cucina con chef Ernesto Iaccarino, Ornella intervista Livia Iaccarino, la prima sommelier donna in Italia, la signora Iaccarino racconta la bellissima storia della famiglia di Don Alfonso, che ha fatto dell' ospitalità la sua stroria.

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: Ornella Fado si trova a Roma e visita nel cuore di Roma a Piazza Navona il ristorante stellato "Il Convivio di Troiani" dello Chef Massimo Troiani. Insieme allo Chef Daniele Lippi cucinano dei piatti gustosi, quanto raffinati. Ornella intervista Massimo Troiani e partecipa come "USA Ambassador " alla serata di gala "Eccellenze Italiane" presso "La Pergola" dei Cavalieri Hilton, intervistando Il re degli Chef Heniz Beck.

programme.title: My Talented Friends Season 1
programme.desc: Ornella Fado introduce il suo amico Geniale Sergio Furnari, e presenta un docuemntario su un suo ultimo lavoro aritistico "Covid Hero Monument"

programme.title: My Talented Friends Season 1
programme.desc: Ornella Fado, passa una giornata a New York con il suo amico Geniale, il Maestro Antonio Ciacca, e tra una chiacchierata a Lincoln Center ed una passeggiata a Central Park, arrivano al prestigiosto Pier Hotel dove il Maestro Ciacca si esibisce regolarmente. in questo episodio di "MY TALENTED FRIENDS" scoprirete la storia di successo del Maestro Ciacca in un pccolo documentario MY JAZZ LIFE.

programme.title: Il CIRCOLO DEL BUONGUSTAI Con Fabio Campoli
